title: Scaling Detector — Inverting the Scaling Law

top_100_details.name: Numbers p such that p, 2p-1, 3p-2, 4p-3, 5p-4, 6p-5, 7p-6, 8p-7 are primes.
The broken eggs problem.
Numbers that are divisible by exactly 7 distinct primes.
Record (maximal) gaps between prime septuplets (p, p+2, p+8, p+12, p+14, p+18, p+20).
Number of partitions of n into products of 5 primes.
Weight distribution of [127,36,31] primitive binary BCH code.
Weight distribution of [255,63,63] primitive binary BCH code.
Sum of interior angles in an n-sided polygon in degrees.
First differences of A069475, successive differences of (n+1)^6 - n^6.
Numbers containing squares of Pythagorean triples in their divisor set.
Odd bi-unitary abundant numbers: odd numbers k such that bsigma(k) > 2*k, where bsigma is the sum of the bi-unitary divisors function (A188999).
Odd coreful abundant numbers: the odd terms of A308053.
Numbers k such that k, 2*k+1, 3*k+2, 4*k+3, 5*k+4, 6*k+5, 7*k+6 are primes.
Numbers that are divisible by the first, i.e., the leftmost, digit.
Record values in A132085.
Paradigm shift sequence for (0,5) production scheme with replacement.
Paradigm shift sequence for (5,3) production scheme with replacement.
Paradigm shift sequence for (5,5) production scheme with replacement.
Numbers which cannot be written as the sum of 4 distinct proper prime powers (A246547).
Numbers k for which no i > 1 exists such that k + i is composite and its least prime factor exceeds i^2.
Crystal ball sequence for E_8 lattice.
Primes with 48 as smallest positive primitive root.
Expansion of e.g.f. exp(x * (1 + x^4)^(3/4)).
Records for unitary abundant numbers, i.e., those integers which set a record for having a greater unitary abundance than any of their predecessors.
Numbers n that raised to the powers from 1 to k (with k>=1) are multiple of the sum of their digits (n raised to k+1 must not be a multiple). Case k=11.
Number of n-cycles on the graph of the regular 120-cell, 3 <= n <= 600.
Numbers n such that there exists an x!=n that makes {n,n,n,x} an amicable multiset.
Numbers k such that 1/phi(x) + 1/phi(y) = 1/phi(k), for some x + y = k and phi(k) is the Euler totient function of k.
Numbers i such that Fibonacci(i) is divisible by i+k for k=0..5.
Number of 5-colorings of an n-wheel graph.
Infinitary aliquot sequence starting at 840: a(1) = 840, a(n) = A126168(a(n-1)), for n >= 2.
Numbers that are less than the number of their ordered factorizations into squarefree numbers greater than 1.
Primes p in prime quadruplets (p,p+2,p+6,p+8) at the end of maximal gaps in A113404.
Primes of the form 5n^2 + 6.

a(n) equals the smallest Sophie Germain prime q such that pi_(p,2p+1)(q,10,(1,3)) - pi_(p,2p+1)(q,10,(3,1)) = n, where pi_(p,2p+1)(q,10,(b,c)) equals the number of Sophie Germain primes A005384(i) suc
Where records occur in A135381.

Pick any pair of "0" digits in the sequence. Those two "0"s are separated by k digits. This is the lexicographically earliest sequence of distinct terms in which all the resulting values of k are dist
Arrange nonzero digits of n in increasing order then append the zeros.
a(n) is the number of distinct honeycomb cell walls built after the n-th step of the walk described in A355478.
Concatenations of pairs of primes that differ by 100.
Angled numbers with an internal digit as the vertex.
Multiples of 12.
Numbers whose square is the sum of the squares of 11 consecutive integers.
Odd abundant numbers divisible neither by 5 nor 7.
Numbers n such that n^10+10 and n^10-10 are prime.
Primes with digit sum = 49.
Primes congruent to 19 mod 45.
Numbers in which 0 outnumbers all other digits together.
An integer sequence giving a counterexample to a theorem of Szüsz and Volkmann.
a(n) = round(n*phi^17), where phi is the golden ratio, A001622.
Primes p such that (p,p+4) is a pair of cousin primes and p == 7 (mod 10).
Primes p such that p + 4, p + 12 and p + 16 are also primes.
Number of n X 7 0..1 arrays avoiding 0 0 0 and 1 1 1 horizontally and 0 0 1 and 1 0 1 vertically.
Composite numbers which in base 5 contain their largest proper factor as a substring.
Primes of the form 30n - 13.
Primes starting a sequence of 6 consecutive primes with gaps 2, 4, 8, 16, 32.
Primes congruent to 16 mod 63.
Numbers k such that the least term in the periodic part of the continued fraction for sqrt(k) is 30.
16*P_6(n), 16 times the Legendre Polynomial of order 6 at n.
a(n) = 7^n mod 30.
Primes for which the weight as defined in A117078 is 11 and the gap as defined in A001223 is 10.
Start with 1057 and repeatedly reverse the digits and add 2 to get the next term.
Centered 40-gonal numbers.
Icosagonal numbers divisible by 20.
Multiples of 16.
Primes of the form 7n^2 - 1.
Powers of -3: a(n) = (-3)^n.
Numbers with an even number of digits such that the second half is twice the first half.
Number of length 1+5 0..n arrays with no three disjoint pairs in any consecutive six terms having the same sum.
Centered 24-gonal numbers.
Fourth row of Pascal-(1,5,1) array A081580.
Primes congruent to 19 mod 63.
A sequence appearing in the 3-Bratteli diagram, a(n)=3^(n-3)*(16*n-17), n>=3.
Numbers m such that gcd(m, F(m)) = 2, where F(m) denotes the m-th Fibonacci number.
Primes congruent to 5 mod 63.
Continued fraction for sqrt(230).
Number of nX3 arrays of occupancy after each element stays put or moves to some horizontal or antidiagonal neighbor, with every occupancy equal to zero or two.
a(n) = round(n*phi^20), where phi is the golden ratio, A001622.
Positive integers having more base-6 runs of even length than odd.
Centered 44-gonal numbers.

bottom_100_details.name: Decimal expansion of (32*2^(1/4) * (1+sqrt(2)) * exp(-Pi/4) * Gamma(5/4)^4) / Pi^3.
Lexicographically earliest sequence of strictly increasing numbers such that the number of exponential divisors of its partial products is strictly increasing.
Square array A(n,k) = A326128(A388981(n, k)), read by descending antidiagonals.
Decimal expansion of (2 / Pi)^(3/4) * Gamma(3/4)^3.
Decimal expansion of (1/512) / Pi^(9/4) / Gamma(3/4)^3.
Decimal expansion of the dodecahedral root of 2.
Numbers k such that A048146(k)+A162296(k) < 2*k, where A048146 is the sum of non-unitary divisors of n, and A162296 is the sum of divisors of n that have a square factor.
Arrangement of numbers such that every digit rotation of the number n is included in ascending order after those of n-1.
Irregular triangle T(n, k) read by rows, giving on row n all A389465(n) natural numbers in range [n, sigma(n)] that are coprime to n.
G.f. satisfies A(x) = A(x^2 + x^3)/x^2 - 1.
Array read by ascending antidiagonals: A(n, k) = Sum_{j=0..floor(n/k)} n!/((j!)^k*(n - j*k)!) for k > 0 and A(n, 0) = 1.
Length of the longest possible eventual period of the win/lose state of a subtraction game where the largest element of the subtraction set is n.
a(n) = Sum_{k=0..floor(5*n/14)} binomial(k,5*n-14*k).
Number of subsets of {1..n} not forming a finite arithmetic progression.
G.f. A(x) satisfies A(x) = 1 + x*(1+x^3)^3*A(x)^3.
a(n) = 1 if n is neither squarefree nor powerful, otherwise 0.

Irregular triangle read by rows: row n lists all the quadruples (a,b,c,d) such that a!*b!*c!*d! is a perfect square, with 1 <= a < b < c < d = A387184(n). Within a row, quadruples are sorted by increa
Number of perfect overpartitions of 2*n-1: A390030(2*n-1).
Number of edge-weighted n-regular multigraphs on 2 unlabeled vertices with total edge weight n.
Numbers m such that A390254(m) < c*(4/3)^m, where c = lim_{m->oo} A390254(m)/(4/3)^m = A390321.
a(n) is the number of positive integers with n divisors and no prime divisor greater than n.
Triangle read by rows: number of heapable permutations of length n that contain the number 2 at position k (positions starting from 0).
a(1) = 1 thereafter a(n) = floor(k(n)^n) where k(n) = a(1).a(2)a(3)...a(n-1) = 1.111... is the decimal digit concatenation of all preceding terms.
Upper (1/3,1/2) midsequence of (n^2) and (n^3); see Comments.
Numbers for which the square of its largest prime factor equals the sum of the squares of its other prime factors.
Decimal expansion of the fourth smallest dihedral angle, in radians, in a sphenomegacorona (Johnson solid J_88).
Decimal expansion of 72 / log(7).
Triangle read by rows: T(n,r) is maximal such that there exists a family F of subsets of {1,...,n} of size T(n,r) such that the intersection of no two sets in F has r elements.
Decimal expansion of cos(5*Pi/33).
The sum of the integers k from 1 to n such that gcd(n, k) is a 3-smooth number (A003586).
Number of cyclically symmetric plane partitions with n orbits of cubes up to the action of C3.
Triangle read by rows: T(n,k) is the number of sets of noncrossing paths of size k that cover n nodes arranged in a circle with one node paths disallowed, 0 <= k <= floor(n/2).
Triangle read by rows: T(n,k) is the number of sets of noncrossing paths of size k that cover n nodes arranged in a circle with one node paths allowed, 0 <= k <= n.
Square array read by upward diagonals: A(n,k) is the position of the k-th occurrence of the decimal string n in the infinite Champernowne word 123456789101112... (A033307).
Triangle read by rows: T(n,k) = binomial(n+1,k+1) * binomial(3*n-2*k+1,k) / (n+1), 0<=k<=n.
Triangle read by rows: T(n,k) = 9*(q+1)*q/2 + (3*q+2)*(n mod 3) - 3*k + 3 for 1 <= k <= n, where q = floor(n/3).
Decimal expansion of the largest dihedral angle, in radians, in a disphenocingulum (Johnson solid J_90).
a(n) = floor( prime(n) / floor(log(prime(n))) ), where prime(n) is the n-th prime.
Numbers k > 1 such that the least prime index of k plus the greatest prime index of k is even.
Number of integer partitions of n > 0 such that the least and greatest parts are not both odd (equivalently, their product is even).
Expansion of g^2/(1 + x^3*g^4), where g = 1+x*g^3 is the g.f. of A001764.
Numbers k such that Stern polynomial B(3*k,x) is not equal to B(3,x) * B(k,x).
Site percolation series for triangular lattice: coefficients of the power series expansion in powers of q=1-p of the probability that a given open site belongs to the infinite cluster, where p is the
Number of compositions of n where the median of parts equals 1.
Positive integer multipliers m such that each integer can be written as a sum of progressive powers 5^t multiplied by coefficients in (-m,-1,0,1,m).
Decimal expansion of the dihedral angle, in radians, between two adjacent pentagonal faces (meeting at their longest edge) in a Dürer's solid.
Numbers k such that 7*k = A048720(m, k) for some m, where A048720 is carryless base-2 multiplication.
Number of pairs {j,k}, 0 < j < k < n, such that j*n + k and k*n + j are distinct and have the same squarefree kernel.
Decimal expansion of Sum_{k>=1} eta(2*k) * Fibonacci(2*k) / 5^k, where eta is the Dirichlet eta function.
Numbers m such that the product m*(m+1) has a set of prime divisors, from greatest down to 2, that is missing exactly one prime divisor.
Decimal expansion of the surface area of an Escher's solid with unit shorter edge length.
Decimal expansion of (7*sqrt(5)-5)/22.
The smallest nonnegative number k such that n! does not contain k as a substring.
Triangle read by rows: row n gives T(n,k) = Sum_{j=1..n} #{m <= j : A001222(m)=k} for k=1..floor(log_2(n)).
Numbers whose square is the concatenation of two or more powers of 2.
For each starting value n, the number of distinct terms in the sequence obtained by repeatedly doubling and then deleting repeated digits (keeping the first occurrence) until a term repeats.

Array read by antidiagonals: Place k points in general position on each side of a regular n-gon and join every pair of the k*n boundary points by a chord; T(n,k) (n >= 3, k >= 0) gives the number of v
a(n) = Sum_{k=0..floor(3*n/5)} binomial(k,3*n-5*k).
Numbers k such that 25*2^k + 9 is prime.
Sum of odd-indexed parts of the reversed n-th composition in standard order.
a(n) = Sum_{k=0..floor(n/4)} binomial(2*n-5*k,3*k).
Decimal expansion of the number whose continued fraction coefficients are given in A072193.
Sum of the largest parts in the partitions of n into 3 parts whose smallest part is even.
Positive integers (not multiples of 10) whose constant congruence speed is smaller than the sum of the constant congruence speeds of all their prime factors.
Numbers k such that A322582(k) <= A276086(k) <= A348507(k).
Number of nonzero digits in the primorial base representation of n (A049345) such that their corresponding radix prime divides n.
a(n) is the smallest k such that omega(k*n) = omega(k + n) where omega = A001221.
Number of reversed integer partitions of n with all distinct 0-based partial alternating sums.
Negated least odd-indexed part in row n of A391983 (the 0-based partial alternating sums of the n-th composition in standard order).
The z values of solutions to x + y = z with x >= y, x and y coprime, and the distinct prime factors of x*y*z are consecutive primes starting with 2.
Decimal expansion of e^(9/2).
Number of trailing zeros in the primorial base representation of the arithmetic derivative of n.
Number of divisors d of n such that d and A276086(d) are coprime, where A276086 is the primorial base exp-function.
Decimal expansion of 5/36 - log(2)/6.

Irregular triangle read by rows: T(n,k) is the number of ways to partition n labeled elements into pie slices whose part sizes are given by the k-th partition of n, allowing the pie to be turned over,
Iteration numbers k for which MD5^k(128 bits 1) yields record lows.
a(n) is the number of partitions of A005101(n) into distinct abundant numbers.
Decimal expansion of the weight factor for Hermite-Gauss quadrature of degree 7 corresponding to abscissa A393369.
Array read by antidiagonals: T(n,k) is the number of one-sided (r,s)-polyleapers with k cells, where r = A094192(n-1) and s = A094193(n-1) if n > 1, (r,s) = (1,0) if n = 1; n, k >= 1.
Inverse permutation to A393423.
For k >= 1, perfect powers A001597(k) for which ratio A001597(k+1) / A001597(k) is smaller than that for any smaller k.
Number k such that k and k+1 are both s-practical numbers (A393492).
Number of partitions of n having four times as many even parts as odd.
Number of ways, up to rotations and reflections, to distribute n guards on the corners and walls of an equilateral triangle castle so that each wall has an equal number of guards.
Triangle read by rows, T(n, k) is the number of 3-dimensional balanced ballot paths of 3n steps and semisymmetric height of exactly 2k, where the semisymmetric height of point (x_1, x_2, x_3, x_4) is
Greatest integer b such that the sum of the integer digits of n in bases 2..b strictly increases.
a(n) = number of partitions of n into distinct parts that are triangular numbers, no two of which are consecutive triangular numbers.
a(n) is the number of unit cubes in the cubic lattice, divided by 8, that have 7 vertices strictly inside and 1 vertex strictly outside the sphere x^2 + y^2 + z^2 = n.
Decimal expansion of Product_{p prime} (1 - (p-1)^2 * (p^2+1) / p^6).
a(n) is the number of points that have been added when n collinear points first appear in the path defined in A393932.
Decimal expansion of Product_{p prime} ((1 - 1/(p+1)^2) * (1 - 1/(p^2+1)^2)).
Decimal expansion of 1/zeta(2)^2 + Sum_{k>=2} k * ((1/zeta(k)-1/zeta(k+1))^2).
Decimal expansion of 15015/(1382*Pi^2).
Numbers k such that the sum of distinct prime factors and the sum of their exponents are divisible in at least one direction, while the corresponding products are divisible in neither direction.

Decimal expansion of the average reciprocal of Euclidean distance between two randomly picked points, one of which is located in a unit cube and the other in an adjacent unit cube, both axis-parallel,

Decimal expansion of the average reciprocal of Euclidean distance between two randomly picked points, one of which is located in a unit cube and the other in an adjacent unit cube, both axis-parallel,
Decimal expansion of the negated value of Sum_{n>=1} cos(n)/n^(1/3).
Decimal expansion of the constant characterizing the complexity of the special number field sieve (SNFS) factoring algorithm.
